title: Dresser with Open Bottom Shelf (Open-Leg Version)
summary: A five-drawer dresser on four exposed 1x2/1x3 corner legs rather than an enclosed panel base — the open-bottom shelf sits between visible stick legs instead of a closed carcass.
description:
Where this catalog's other open-bottom-shelf dresser is built as a single enclosed plywood box on a hidden 2x2 frame, this version stands on four genuinely exposed legs — two 1x2 side legs and two 1x3 front/back legs at each corner, left visible rather than boxed in. That's a real structural difference, not a cosmetic one: the drawer housing (the partitioned box holding the five drawers) sits on top of this open leg framework rather than being part of one continuous carcass.

Build the drawer housing first — its own shelves, partitions, and sides — then trim every edge as you go, cutting trim pieces to fit rather than trusting the cut list blind, since a housing this size accumulates small variances. The optional 1/4" plywood back can be omitted entirely if you want the piece to stay fully open at the back; the plan works structurally either way.

Three small drawers fill the top row and two large drawers fill the bottom, all cut to fit the actual finished housing rather than a fixed dimension — measure your own openings before cutting the drawer parts, since the openings depend on how tight your trim ended up.

materials.name: 3/4" MDF or plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet
Stain, paint, varnish, or other finishing supplies

cutList.material: 3/4" MDF or plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet
3/4" MDF or plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Build the drawer housing
steps.body: Assemble the shelves, partitions, and sides into the drawer housing box.
materials: 3/4" MDF or plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Trim the housing
steps.body: Cut and attach the side, top, shelf, and partition trim pieces as you go, fitting each to the actual assembled housing.

steps.title: Build the open-leg base
steps.body: Assemble the four side legs and four front/back legs into an open leg frame, then attach the top and bottom shelves to it.
materials: 3/4" MDF or plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Mount the housing on the base
steps.body: Set the trimmed drawer housing onto the open-leg base and fasten securely.

steps.title: Add the optional back
steps.body: Attach the back panel if a closed-back look is wanted, or leave it open.

steps.title: Build the drawers
steps.body: Measure your own finished openings, then build the three top drawers and two bottom drawers to fit, running on the drawer guides.
materials: 3/4" MDF or plywood, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Add hardware and finish
steps.body: Attach drawer pulls, fill any gaps with wood filler, sand, and apply stain, paint, or varnish.
materials: Stain, paint, varnish, or other finishing supplies

unresolvedImages.alt: Dresser with open bottom shelf and open exposed legs
